title: IHC staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cnhkl","text":"This is standard immunohistochemistry (IHC) procedure recommended in the Bendall and Angelo lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"63vau","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"91l3v","text":"Staining tissue sections is fairly straightforward but there are a few things to be cognizant of when preparing samples:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c5o0j","text":"Always try to limit mechanical damage to the sample surface. This can occur when moving the samples with forceps, particularly when the mounting substrates are small.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"og6k","text":"Once the samples have been rehydrated, they cannot dry out until the end of the protocol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3u92n","text":"Be careful at all times to not touch the tissue, in order to not leave any residue.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"10s52","text":"Once the samples have been stained, fixed, and dehydrated, they have an indefinite shelf life and can be imaged at any time.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bah89","text":"The ImmPRESS Reagent is ready-to-use. No mixing or tittering of the ImmPRESS reagent is necessary to obtain immunohistochemical staining.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d88nq","text":"For optimal performance, the ImmPRESS reagent should be equilibrated to room temperature before use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ofr0","text":"Unused working solution is stable up to 14 days if stored at 2-8ᵒC. Working solution may change color during storage but this will have no effect on the quality or intensity of the staining.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6pr36","text":"Caution: It is important that you do not use normal serum from the primary antibody source, as serum proteins from this source will be recognized by your secondary antibody – making your background staining worse, not better. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7g3lq","text":"Verify the stocks of all reagents and place an order or prepare solutions, if some reagents are running low. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"325ka","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"5bnbl","text":"All organic solvents should be manipulated under a chemical hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Bake the sections at 70 °C for 00:20:00 in an dry incubator Optional : 01:00:00 ; Overnight last 10 min place the slide vertical with the label side up to allow the paraffin to drip down Note: Some tissues or section size may need longer baking time. Recommended to bake at least 1 hour for brain tissue or TMA. This can be extended to 16 h (overnight) .

Prepare Target retrieval solution 2.5 mL of target retrieval solution 10x (3-in-1) in 22.5 mL of Ultrapure TYPE 1 water A B C 1 Total volume (mL) Volume (mL) Target retrieval Volume (mL) H2O 2 25 2.5 22.5 3 50 5 45 4 100 10 90

Put in the containers with the diluted target retrieval solution in the PT Module Thermo ScientificTM Lab VisionTM PT Module (Programmed Water bath, Thermofisher, A80400012, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/A80400012#/A80400012 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/private/42044eb51b7c62802cf5fafe2e207be573165b7082223f1a083d341ba8ce8478/b696bcqzf.png )
PT Module Preheat Press RUN on digital screen and check for PREHEAT 75 on display

Linear Stainer Pour out reagent containers and fill with fresh reagents: Xylene 3x, 100% Ethanol 2x, 95% Ethanol 2x, 80% Ethanol 1x, 70% Ethanol 1x, ddH2O 2x, exit stainless steel tank = ddH2O IMPORTANT : Use fresh xylene for every deparaffination Leica ST4020 Small Linear stainer (Stainer, Leica, 14050946425, https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/private/42044eb51b7c62802cf5fafe2e207be573165b7082223f1a083d341ba8ce8478/b7ambcqzf.png )
Insert slides into slide carriers Place the slide carrier into first xylene container

Press on Menu Check for Processing time = 30 sec, Lift bar = 976, Number of dips = 3 Set Start position corresponding to the first slide carrier position Exemple: If the first slide carrier is at position 4, use Plus ( + ) or Minus ( -) button to increase or decrease to get Start at: 04
Then press enter
Synchronize when the PT module temperature has reached 75˚C then Press Run on the Linear Stainer
Allow the rehydration process and wait until the slides reached the stainless steel tank and stop
Bring the stainless steel tank with the slides close to the PT module
Press run again and check for first WARMUP then HEAT on display
Allow to run for 40 min at 97 °C and then cool down for approximately 50 min and reach 65 °C
When the alarm sounds Stop the PT module
Take out the slides and let cool down at room temperature for at least 00:05:00
Quenching Horseradish Peroxidase (HRP)
Prepare two Coplin jars filled with 1x PBS wash buffer
Open the PT Module and insert the slides in the Target retrieval solution container
Make a 1:10 dilution of 30% Hydrogen peroxide H 2 O 2 A B C 1 Total volume Ultrapure TYPE 1 water (mL) Hydrogen peroxide (mL) 2 1 mL 0.9 0.1 3 5 mL 4.5 0.500 4 50 mL 45 5 mL
Incubate for 30 min by adding diluted H 2 O 2 >100 µL (18 mm 2 ) per sections A B C D E F 1 Estimated Surface area (mm) 10x10 15x15 18x18 20x20 20x45 2 Volume (µL) 50 70 100 150 350
Prepare a Coplin jar filled with 1x PBS wash buffer
Transfer the slides in the first 1x PBS wash buffer and use orbital shaker set for 5 min, 70 rpm
Transfer the slides to the second 1x PBS wash buffer and use orbital shaker set for 5 min, 70 rpm
Transfer quenched tissue/ slides to the 1x PBS wash buffer and use orbital skaker set for 5 min, 70 rpm
Add 100 µL of Blocking Buffer for 18 mm 2 A B C D E F 1 Estimated Surface area (mm) 10x10 15x15 18x18 20x20 20x45 2 Volume (µL) 50 70 100 150 350
Make sure to dry with a folded-tissue paper the slide, leaving a square of wet surface surrounding of the tissue section Note: Do not let dry the tissue section
Draw a square following the outside edges of the wet square with an hydrophibic ink pen (ImmEdge pen, Vector labs)
Place the slides in a moist chamber at Room temperature and incubate 00:20:00 to 01:00:00
Proceed to step 22
Quenching Alkaline Phosphatase
Make a 1:100 dilution of levamisole A B C 1 Total volume Immpact diluent (mL) Levamisol (mL) 2 1 mL 0.990 0.01 3 5 mL 4.950 0.50
Remove the blocking solution by tapping the slide on a side and immediately add the primary antibody Refer to the chart for the volume of antibody to apply %s %s
Dilute the primary antibody in antibody diluent buffer
Place the moist chamber at 4˚C Overnight , preferably in a place with low disturbance (e.g. a designated area in a cold room)
Incubate for 30 min by adding diluted Levamisole >100 µL (18 mm 2 ) per sections A B C D E F 1 Estimated Surface area (mm) 10x10 15x15 18x18 20x20 20x45 2 Volume (µL) 50 70 100 150 350
Prepare two Coplin jars filled with 1x PBS wash buffer
Transfer the slides into the first Coplin jar and use orbital skaker set for 5 min, 70 rpm
Transfer the slides into the second Coplin jar and use orbital skaker set for 5 min, 70 rpm
Transfer quenched tissue/ slides to the 1x PBS wash buffer and use orbital skaker set for 5 min, 70 rpm
Verify stock of Antibody Diluent (3% NHS) and prepare accordingly if running low (Refer MIBI and IHC solutions protocol)

Verify stock of 1x PBS wash buffer and prepare accordingly if running low A B 1 Reagents Qty for 1000 mL 2 PBS IHC Wash Buffer with Tween 20 (mL) 50 3 Bovine Albumin (BSA), heat shock treated (g) 1 4 Ultrapure (type 1) water (mL) 949
Prepare a Coplin jar filled with 1x PBS wash buffer
Add adequate volume of universal anti-mouse/ anti-rabbit (Vector labs, MP-7500-50) Note: 1 drop is ~40 µL %s %s
Place sample in a humidity chamber, transfer to Room temperature , and incubate 00:30:00
go to step #28 than go to step 32
Add 1 drop of chromogenic solution (DAB) in 1 mL dilution buffer (ImmPACT DAB Peroxidase (HRP) Substrate, SK-4105, Vector lab)
Equilibrate at Room temperature the chromogenic substrate and revealing buffer
Take the slide from 1x PBS wash buffer
Place the slide on a absorbant meterial (preferably white)
At 10 s count up, add rapidly the solution in excess over tissue section; starting with the lowest titer
Start a count up timer and take immediatly 1mL DAB diluted solution with 1 mL pipette

Stop at 50 s count up by tapping the side of the slide to remove DAB and putting the slide back in the wash buffer DAB chromogenic reaction is revealed by a brown coloration in positive areas. Optimal revelation time ranges between 30s to 1 min. Rapid brown apperance (<15s) after adding the DAB indicates an over titration of the primary antibody. Light brown or no coloration >1 min reveals is undertitered primary antibody or negative result. Revealling time should be set constant at 40s but can be extented up to 1 min max. Optimal titer at constant development time ensure consistency when large number of slides are processed.

Optional Chromogenic development can be performed directly under a microscope. This should be done at low magnification using a 5 x or a 10x objective. This way, it helps to cover a large field of view where positive areas can be observed. At higher magnification ( e.g . 40x), it is likely that the DAB solution will contaminate the objective and cause a permanent stain if not cleaned immediately with ROR (Residual Oil Remover, EMS, 74319-35) and lens paper. This procedure can be used for single preliminary test but is not recommended for large scale. Importantly, make sure to clean the objectives and stage after use. Otherwise, DAB residues will stain permanently the surface in contact and increase deterioration of the microscope parts in the long term.
Prepare a 1L beaker filled with tap water in a sink
Set staining area by protecting counter top with an absorbant

Add Hematoxylin to cover all tissue sections (Hematoxylin Solution, Harris Modified, Sigma-Aldrich, HHS16-500ML) After 10s stop by dipping and rinsing in the tap water beaker Reveal one slide at the time
Start a count up timer
Add 1x Scott's tap water for 10s, until blue color is revealed
Put in 70% ethanol
Prepare the linear stainer Place the reagents in the following order: 70% Ethanol 1x, 80% Ethanol 1x, 95% Ethanol 2x, 100% Ethanol 2x, exit stainless steel tank = empty
Prepare the linear stainer Place the reagents in the following order: 70% Ethanol 1x, 80% Ethanol 1x, 95% Ethanol 2x, 100% Ethanol 2x, Xylene 3x, exit stainless steel tank = empty
Let air dry for at least 00:02:00

Add 2 drops of mounting media on tissue area by squeezing down the bottle gently for Cytoseal 60 or ~70 µL of ACRYTOL from a small aliquot put in a 50 mL tube Cytoseal 60 VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #48212-154 ACRYTOL mounting medium Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #13518 Work under a fume hood. The mounting media contains toluene as solvent.
Let air dry for at least 00:02:00
Place a glass coverslip no. 1.5 (Thermo Scientific, Gold Seal, coverslips, #1.5, Fisher Scientific, 12-518-108A)
Add 2 drops of mounting media on tissue area using a transfer pipette or a pointed cotton swab VectaMount permanent Vector Laboratories Catalog #H-5000
Place a glass coverslip no. 1.5 (Thermo Scientific, Gold Seal, coverslips, #1.5, Fisher Scientific, 12-518-108A)
Bake at 70 °C for 01:00:00 prior long term storage
Let dry 00:15:00 under fume prior long term storage
Dip 3x the slide quickly in xylene prior mounting
Excess of mounting media can be removed by dipping back the slide in xylene and wiping excess along the slide edges
creator: Marc Bosse
